raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The COVID-19 pandemic drained $12 billion from hospitals.",
    "Skyrocketing prices for pharmaceutical drugs, staff, and medical supplies have put many hospitals on the brink of collapse.",
    "Experts warn that dozens of hospitals across California are at risk of closing or declaring bankruptcy without immediate action.",
    "The California State Legislature created the Distressed Hospital Loan Program, funded with $150 million to provide loans to not-for-profit and public hospitals in significant financial distress.",
    "The Distressed Hospital Loan Program will require financially distressed hospitals to provide more transparency about their financial condition.",
    "The Distressed Hospital Loan Program will create more accountability to ensure the long-term sustainability of hospital operations.",
    "The Distressed Hospital Loan Program will require state verification of financial plans prior to any loan approval.",
    "The hospital closure in Madera and other looming closures would be catastrophic in both rural and urban communities.",
    "California has established itself as a national leader in expanding healthcare options."
  ]
}
```
